name: White Sauce Mix
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Instant Nonfat Dry Milk OR 1 1/2 cups Regular Non Fat Dry Milk
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the dry milk, flour, and salt. Mix well.
2. Add the butter or margarine to the dry ingredients. Using a pastry blender, cut it in until the mixture resembles fine crumbs.
3. Pack the mixture into a large, airtight container. Label it White Sauce Mix and store in the refrigerator. Use within 2 months. It makes about 1 quart of mix.
To Make Basic White Sauce:
1. In a small saucepan, combine 1/2 cup of the White Sauce Mix with 1 cup of cool water. For a thinner sauce, use 1/4 cup; for a thicker sauce, use 3/4 cup.
2. Cook over low heat until smooth, stirring constantly. Season with pepper, herbs, and spices as desired. This makes about 1 1/2 cups of sauce.
Variations:
- Substitute milk, tomato juice, chicken or beef stock for all or part of the water.
- Cheese Sauce: After the mixture thickens, add 1/2 to 1 cup of shredded Cheddar cheese and stir until melted.
- Curry Sauce: Add 1 tablespoon of curry powder after the mixture has thickened.
